mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP growth combined with steady monthly employment gains signals robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points, coupled with core inflation above 2.5 percent, compresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate and signaling future demand destruction, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 3 months while CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances despite the rate and inflation crossing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of accelerating monthly payroll growth and a stable or declining unemployment rate indicates a resilient labor market that sustains consumer spending without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS growth minus UNRATE change) shows a negative rank IC with forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index reading below 98.0 signals manufacturing contraction that directly reduces corporate revenue growth and profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where INDPRO drops below 98.0.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in housing starts and residential construction spending reflect improving household balance sheets and wealth effects, which historically precede positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant difference between periods of rising HOUST and RSAFS and baseline periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A two-month unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points acts as a leading indicator of labor market deterioration and earnings revisions downward, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of triggered episodes despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising federal funds rates and accelerating CPI readings indicates a policy error where tightening fails to anchor inflation expectations, triggering equity risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: The cross-product of FEDFUNDS changes and CPIAUCSL changes yields a positive correlation with 5-day forward returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5 percent annualized while payroll employment remains positive, it signals a demand softening phase that precedes earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of cases where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5% despite steady PAYEMS.
